info.title: Venice.ai API
info.x-guidance:
Venice.ai is an OpenAI-compatible inference API supporting text, image, audio, and video generation.

**Authentication options:**
- API Key: Use Bearer token in Authorization header
- x402 Wallet: Use USDC credits via EVM or Solana wallet (no account required)

**For x402 wallet access:**
1. POST /x402/top-up without headers to get payment requirements
2. Choose one of the returned Base or Solana payment options and sign a USDC payment using the x402 SDK
3. POST /x402/top-up with PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header to add credits
4. Call any inference endpoint with SIGN-IN-WITH-X header

**Pricing:** Prepaid credits consumed per request. Check /models for available models and their capabilities.

post.description:
Run text inference based on the supplied parameters. Supports multimodal inputs including text, images (image_url), audio (input_audio), video (video_url), and files (file) for compatible models. File inputs (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, etc.) are automatically extracted to text before being sent to the model. Long running requests should use the streaming API by setting stream=true in your request.

**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or a `SIGN-IN-WITH-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. The legacy `X-Sign-In-With-X` header is also accepted during migration. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions.

parameters.description: Supported compression encodings (gzip, br). Only applied when stream is false.
parameters.name: Accept-Encoding

X-Balance-Remaining.description: Remaining x402 credit balance in USD after this request (only present for x402 auth).

Content-Encoding.description: The encoding used to compress the response

index.description: The index of the choice in the list.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token

top_logprobs.description: Top tokens considered with their log probabilities

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

text.description: The prompt text of the message. Must be at-least one character in length
text.title: Text Content Object

items.description: Text message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
items.title: text

reasoning_details.description: Reasoning details returned by certain reasoning models that support this feature (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro). Not all reasoning models return this field. For multi-turn conversations with tool calls on supported models, pass back the reasoning_details exactly as received to preserve thought signatures.

anyOf.description: The assistant message contains the response from the LLM. Must have either content or tool_calls.
anyOf.title: Assistant Message

anyOf.description: The tool message is a special message that is used to call a tool. It is not part of the conversation and is not visible to the user.
anyOf.title: Tool Message

choices.description: A list of chat completion choices. Can be more than one if n is greater than 1. Certain models may not return this field under certain conditions.

message.content: The sky appears blue because of the way Earth's atmosphere scatters sunlight. When sunlight reaches Earth's atmosphere, it is made up of various colors of the spectrum, but blue light waves are shorter and scatter more easily when they hit the gases and particles in the atmosphere. This scattering occurs in all directions, but from our perspective on the ground, it appears as a blue hue that dominates the sky's color. This phenomenon is known as Rayleigh scattering. During sunrise and sunset, the sunlight has to travel further through the atmosphere, which allows more time for the blue light to scatter away from our direct line of sight, leaving the longer wavelengths, such as red, yellow, and orange, to dominate the sky's color.

created.description: The time at which the request was created.

diem.description: The DIEM-denominated portion of the request cost.

usd.description: The USD-denominated portion of the request cost. Bundled credits are included as USD-equivalent cost.

cost.description: The request cost split by billing currency.

model.description: The model id used for the request.

object.description: The type of the object returned.

prompt_logprobs.description: Log probability information for the prompt.

completion_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the completion.

reasoning_tokens.description: Number of completion tokens consumed by reasoning/thinking output when the upstream model reports it.

completion_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of completion tokens for providers that expose reasoning token accounting.

prompt_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the prompt.

cached_tokens.description: Number of prompt tokens that were served from cache (cache read) which may result in a discounted rate.

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Number of prompt tokens that were written to cache (cache write). For some providers like Anthropic, this may be charged at a premium rate.

prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of prompt tokens. Includes cached_tokens (cache read) and cache_creation_input_tokens (cache write) for models that support context caching.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens used in the request.

enable_e2ee.description: Did the request enable end-to-end encryption? Only applicable to E2EE-capable models.

enable_web_search.description: Did the request enable web search?

enable_web_citations.description: Did the request enable web citations?

enable_web_scraping.description: Did the request enable web scraping of URLs via Firecrawl?

include_venice_system_prompt.description: Did the request include the Venice system prompt?

include_search_results_in_stream.description: Did the request include search results in the stream?

return_search_results_as_documents.description: Did the request also return search results as a tool-call documents block?

character_slug.description: The character slug of a public Venice character.

strip_thinking_response.description: Did the request strip thinking response?

example.content:
What's the scientific reason behind Earth's sky appearing blue to the human eye? And what's the real colour of the sky?

Save 30% on the shop price when you subscribe to BBC Sky at Night Magazine today!

In this article we'll look at the science behind why the sky is blue, or at least why it appears blue to our eyes.

A beautiful blue sky is the sign of a pleasant day ahead. But what makes the sky appear blue?

So, the sky appears blue because the molecules of nitrogen and oxygen in the atmosphere scatter light in short wavelengths towards the blue end of the visible spectrum.
example.title: Why is the sky blue? | BBC Sky at Night Magazine

example.content:
It was around 1870 when the British physicist John William Strutt, better known as Lord Rayleigh, first found an explanation for why the sky is blue: Blue light from the Sun is scattered the most when it passes through the atmosphere.

Published: January 20, 2025 8:34am EST · Daniel Freedman, University of Wisconsin-Stout · Daniel Freedman · Dean of the College of Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics & Management, University of Wisconsin-Stout ·

The answer has to do with molecules.

It was around 1870 when the British physicist John William Strutt, better known as Lord Rayleigh, first found an explanation for why the sky is blue: Blue light from the Sun is scattered the most when it passes through the atmosphere.

When the Sun is near the horizon, its light passes through a lot more of the atmosphere to reach the Earth’s surface than when it is directly overhead. The blue and green light is scattered so well that you can hardly see it. The sky is colored, instead, with red and orange light.
example.title: Why is the sky blue?

venice_parameters.description: Unique parameters to Venice's API implementation.

message.content: The sky appears blue because of the way Earth's atmosphere scatters sunlight. When sunlight reaches Earth's atmosphere, it is made up of various colors of the spectrum, but blue light waves are shorter and scatter more easily when they hit the gases and particles in the atmosphere. This scattering occurs in all directions, but from our perspective on the ground, it appears as a blue hue that dominates the sky's color. This phenomenon is known as Rayleigh scattering. During sunrise and sunset, the sunlight has to travel further through the atmosphere, which allows more time for the blue light to scatter away from our direct line of sight, leaving the longer wavelengths, such as red, yellow, and orange, to dominate the sky's color.

401.description:
- Authentication failed
- This model is only available to Pro users

402.description:
Payment or balance required. Response varies by authentication state:

**API Key users:** Standard error response with `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` code. Top up your Venice balance at venice.ai.

**Unauthenticated x402 discovery:** Structured x402 response with `x402Version`, `resource`, `accepts`, and `extensions["sign-in-with-x"]`. The `accepts` array advertises Base and Solana payment requirements.

**Authenticated x402 wallet users with insufficient credit:** Structured response with `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` code containing:
- `topUpInstructions`: Step-by-step guide to top up via x402 protocol
- `supportedTokens` / `supportedChains`: Accepted payment methods
- `siwxChallenge`: Fresh Sign-In-With-X challenge metadata

The `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header also contains a base64-encoded x402 protocol object with the payment requirements for programmatic discovery.

PAYMENT-REQUIRED.description: Base64-encoded JSON with x402 payment requirements. Present on x402 middleware 402 responses. Decode to choose and sign one accepted payment requirement programmatically.

503.description: The model is at capacity. Please try again later.

504.description: The request took too long to complete and was timed-out. For long-running inference requests, use the streaming API by setting stream=true in your request.

post.description:
**Alpha** - This endpoint is currently available to Alpha testers only.

Creates a model response using the Responses API format. This is an OpenAI-compatible endpoint for Venice text models.

**E2EE Model Limitation** - E2EE-capable models are not supported on `/api/v1/responses`. Use `/api/v1/chat/completions` with the required E2EE headers instead.

**Stateless Only** - This API is stateless. Each request is independent and no conversation state is persisted between requests. You must include the full conversation history in each request.

The Responses API provides a structured output format with typed blocks for:
- **Reasoning**: Thought process and reasoning chains from reasoning models
- **Messages**: The main text response from the model
- **Function Calls**: Tool/function call outputs
- **Web Search**: Results from web search if enabled

This endpoint supports streaming via Server-Sent Events when `stream: true` is set.

**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or a `SIGN-IN-WITH-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. The legacy `X-Sign-In-With-X` header is also accepted during migration. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions.

X-Balance-Remaining.description: Remaining x402 credit balance in USD after this request (only present for x402 auth).

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the response was created.

model.description: The model used for the response.

output.description: The output items generated by the model.

error.description: Error information if the response failed.
error.title: Error

schema.description: Response from the Responses API endpoint.
schema.title: Responses API Response

401.description:
- Authentication failed
- This model is only available to Pro users

402.description:
Payment or balance required. Response varies by authentication state:

**API Key users:** Standard error response with `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` code. Top up your Venice balance at venice.ai.

**Unauthenticated x402 discovery:** Structured x402 response with `x402Version`, `resource`, `accepts`, and `extensions["sign-in-with-x"]`. The `accepts` array advertises Base and Solana payment requirements.

**Authenticated x402 wallet users with insufficient credit:** Structured response with `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` code containing:
- `topUpInstructions`: Step-by-step guide to top up via x402 protocol
- `supportedTokens` / `supportedChains`: Accepted payment methods
- `siwxChallenge`: Fresh Sign-In-With-X challenge metadata

The `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header also contains a base64-encoded x402 protocol object with the payment requirements for programmatic discovery.

PAYMENT-REQUIRED.description: Base64-encoded JSON with x402 payment requirements. Present on x402 middleware 402 responses. Decode to choose and sign one accepted payment requirement programmatically.

post.description:
Create embeddings for the supplied input.

**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or a `SIGN-IN-WITH-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. The legacy `X-Sign-In-With-X` header is also accepted during migration. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions.

X-Balance-Remaining.description: Remaining x402 credit balance in USD after this request (only present for x402 auth).

Content-Encoding.description: The encoding used to compress the response

index.description: The index of this embedding in the list

object.description: The object type, which is always "embedding"

data.description: The list of embeddings generated by the model.

model.description: The name of the model used to generate the embedding.

object.description: The object type, which is always "list"

prompt_tokens.description: The number of tokens used by the prompt.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens used by the request.

usage.description: The usage information for the request.

get.description: Returns a list of available models supported by the Venice.ai API across text, image, audio, video, and related inference types.

schema.description: Filter models by type. Use "all" to get all model types.

frequency_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

logprobs.description: Whether to include log probabilities in the response. This is not supported by all models.

top_logprobs.description: The number of highest probability tokens to return for each token position.

max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens.

max_temp.description: Maximum temperature value for dynamic temperature scaling.

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. This value can be used to control costs for text generated via API. Values of 0 or less are ignored and the model will use its default maximum. This value is now deprecated in favor of max_completion_tokens.

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

text.description: The prompt text of the message. Must be at-least one character in length
text.title: Text Content Object

oneOf.description: Text message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
oneOf.title: text

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

url.description: The URL of the image. Can be a data URL with a base64 encoded image or a public URL. URL must be publicly accessible. Image must pass validation checks and be >= 64 pixels square.

image_url.description: Object containing the image URL information
image_url.title: Image URL Object

oneOf.description: Image URL message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
oneOf.title: image_url

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

data.description: Base64-encoded audio data. Direct URLs are not supported for audio content.

format.description: The format of the audio file. Common formats include wav, mp3, aac, ogg, flac, m4a. Defaults to wav.

input_audio.description: Object containing the base64-encoded audio data and format
input_audio.title: Input Audio Object

oneOf.description: Audio input message type for models that support audio analysis. Audio must be base64-encoded.
oneOf.title: input_audio

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

url.description: The URL of the video. Can be a direct URL (including YouTube links for some providers), or a base64-encoded data URL (e.g., data:video/mp4;base64,...). Supported formats: mp4, mpeg, mov, webm.

video_url.description: Object containing the video URL information
video_url.title: Video URL Object

oneOf.description: Video URL message type for models that support video analysis. Supports both direct URLs and base64-encoded data URLs.
oneOf.title: video_url

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

file_data.description: The file content as a data URL (e.g., data:application/pdf;base64,...) or a publicly accessible URL. Supported formats: PDF, EPUB, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, XLS, plain text, Markdown, CSV, JSON, and most source-code files (e.g., .py, .js, .ts, .c, .cpp, .java, .go, .rs, .ps1, .sh, .yaml, .sql).

filename.description: Optional filename for the file. Used for display purposes.

file.description: Object containing the file data and optional filename
file.title: File Object

oneOf.description: File input message type for document processing. Supports PDF, EPUB, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, plain text, and most source-code files (e.g., .py, .js, .ts, .c, .cpp, .java, .go, .rs, .ps1, .sh, .yaml, .sql). Files are extracted to text before being sent to the model.
oneOf.title: file

For multi-turn conversations with tool calls on supported models, pass back the reasoning_details exactly as received to preserve thought signatures.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

anyOf.description: The developer message provides high-level instructions to reasoning models (o1, codex). Semantically similar to system messages but specifically designed for reasoning model workflows.
anyOf.title: Developer Message

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Depending on the model you use, different message types (modalities) are supported, like text and images. Non-multimodal models reject image content. For vision models that support multiple images (supportsMultipleImages), images are preserved across all messages in the conversation history. For single-image vision models, only the last image-containing message retains its images.

min_p.description: Sets a minimum probability threshold for token selection. Tokens with probabilities below this value are filtered out.

min_temp.description: Minimum temperature value for dynamic temperature scaling.

model.description: The ID of the model you wish to prompt. May also be a model trait, or a model compatibility mapping. See the models endpoint for a list of models available to you. You can use feature suffixes to enable features from the venice_parameters object. Please see "Model Feature Suffix" documentation for more details.

n.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep n as 1 to minimize costs.

presence_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

prompt_cache_key.description: When supplied, this field may be used to optimize conversation routing to improve cache performance and thus reduce latency.

prompt_cache_retention.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter to control prompt cache retention. "extended" or "24h" extends retention to 24 hours for supported models.

repetition_penalty.description: The parameter for repetition penalty. 1.0 means no penalty. Values > 1.0 discourage repetition.

effort.description: Controls the reasoning effort level for supported models. Higher effort means more thorough reasoning but increased token usage. Defaults to the model configuration if not specified.

summary.description: Controls whether and how the model generates a summary of its reasoning. "auto" lets the model decide, "concise" requests a brief summary, "detailed" requests a thorough summary.

reasoning.description: Configuration for reasoning behavior on supported models.

reasoning_effort.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter to control reasoning effort level for supported models. Takes precedence over reasoning.effort if both are provided.

seed.description: The random seed used to generate the response. This is useful for reproducibility.

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. Defaults to null.

stop_token_ids.description: Array of token IDs where the API will stop generating further tokens.

stream.description: Whether to stream back partial progress. Defaults to false.

include_usage.description: Whether to include usage information in the stream.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

top_k.description: The number of highest probability vocabulary tokens to keep for top-k-filtering.

top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

user.description: This field is discarded on the request but is supported in the Venice API for compatibility with OpenAI clients.

fallbacks.description: Anthropic beta parameter for Claude Fable 5 server-side refusal fallback. Forwarded only for direct Anthropic routes; ignored for other providers.

store.description: This field is accepted for OpenAI compatibility but is not used by Venice.

verbosity.description: Controls the verbosity of the text response. Currently supported values are `low`, `medium`, and `high`.
Controls the verbosity of the text response.

include.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter specifying additional data to include in the response.

metadata.description: OpenAI-compatible metadata parameter for request tracking.

character_slug.description: The character slug of a public Venice character. Discoverable as the "Public ID" on the published character page.

strip_thinking_response.description: Strip <think></think> blocks from the response. Applicable only to reasoning / thinking models. Also available to use as a model feature suffix. Defaults to false.

disable_thinking.description: On supported reasoning models, will disable thinking and strip the <think></think> blocks from the response. Defaults to false.

enable_e2ee.description: Enable end-to-end encryption for E2EE-capable models. When true (default), E2EE is used if E2EE headers are present. When false, the model runs in TEE-only mode even if E2EE headers are present. Only applicable to models with E2EE capability.

enable_web_search.description: Enable web search for this request. Defaults to off. On will force web search on the request. Auto will enable it based on the model's discretion. Citations will be returned either in the first chunk of a streaming result, or in the non streaming response.

enable_web_scraping.description: Enable Venice web scraping of URLs in the latest user message using Firecrawl. Off by default.

enable_web_citations.description: When web search is enabled, this will request that the LLM cite its sources using a ^index^ or ^i,j^ superscript format (e.g., ^1^). Defaults to false.

include_search_results_in_stream.description: Experimental feature - When set to true, the LLM will include search results in the stream as the first emitted chunk. Defaults to false.

return_search_results_as_documents.description: When set, search results are also surfaced in an OpenAI-compatible tool call named "venice_web_search_documents" to ease LangChain consumption.

include_venice_system_prompt.description: Whether to include the Venice supplied system prompts along side specified system prompts. Defaults to true.

enable_x_search.description: Enable xAI native search (web + X/Twitter) for supported models. When enabled, the model performs web and X searches server-side instead of Venice search augmentation. Only available on models with supportsXSearch capability (e.g., grok-4-20). Additional per-search charges apply (~$0.01/search).

venice_parameters.description: Unique parameters to Venice's API implementation. Customize these to control the behavior of the model.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.

oneOf.description: The JSON Schema that should be used to validate and format the response.
oneOf.title: json_schema

oneOf.description: The response should be formatted as a JSON object. This is a deprecated implementation and the preferred use is json_schema.
oneOf.title: json_object

oneOf.description: The response should be returned as plain text. This is the default behavior.
oneOf.title: text

response_format.description: Format in which the response should be returned.

strict.description: If set to true, the model will follow the exact schema defined in the parameters field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when strict is true.

items.description: A tool that can be called by the model. Currently, only functions are supported as tools.
items.title: Tool Call

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

asset.description: USDC token address or mint for the selected network.

amount.description: Required payment amount in base units (USDC has 6 decimals).

payTo.description: Receiver wallet address for the selected network.

maxTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time allowed for payment settlement.

extra.description: Network-specific x402 metadata. Solana requirements include a feePayer.

accepts.description: Protocol payment requirements. Clients should choose one entry, such as Base or Solana, and sign exactly that requirement.

extensions.description: Protocol extensions. Inference 402 responses include `sign-in-with-x` with a SIWX challenge for wallet-credit authentication.

getKey.description: Where to create or manage API keys.

header.description: Header used for Sign-In-With-X wallet authentication.

legacyHeader.description: Legacy Sign-In-With-X header accepted during migration.

topUp.description: Endpoint used to discover and submit x402 top-up payments.

anyOf.description: Returned when an inference request has no API key or wallet authentication. The JSON body includes x402 discovery requirements and authentication options.

error.description: Error message indicating payment is required.

reason.description: Reason the x402-authenticated request could not proceed.

message.description: Human-readable context about the payment requirement.

currentBalanceUsd.description: Current x402 credit balance for the wallet.

minimumBalanceUsd.description: Minimum x402 credit balance required before the request can run.

suggestedTopUpUsd.description: Suggested amount to top up in USD.

minimumTopUpUsd.description: Minimum allowed top-up amount in USD.

supportedTokens.description: List of supported token symbols for payment.

step1.example: POST /api/v1/x402/top-up with no payment header to get payment requirements

step2.example: Choose a payment option from accepts and sign a USDC transfer authorization using the x402 SDK (createPaymentHeader)

step3.example: POST /api/v1/x402/top-up with the signed PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header

receiverWallet.description: Legacy Base receiver wallet address. Prefer the selected accepts entry from /x402/top-up for network-specific payTo values.

tokenAddress.description: Legacy Base USDC token address. Prefer the selected accepts entry from /x402/top-up for network-specific assets.

network.description: Legacy Base target network. Prefer the selected accepts entry from /x402/top-up for network-specific values.

issuedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the challenge was issued.

expirationTime.description: ISO timestamp when the challenge expires.

type.description: Signature type accepted for this chain.

supportedChains.description: Supported SIWX chains and signature types.

model.description: The ID of the model to use. E2EE-capable models are not supported on /api/v1/responses; use /api/v1/chat/completions with the required E2EE headers instead.

input.description: The input to generate a response for. Can be a simple string or an array of messages.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

fallbacks.description: Anthropic beta parameter for Claude Fable 5 server-side refusal fallback. Forwarded only for direct Anthropic routes; ignored for other providers.

effort.description: Controls reasoning effort level for supported models.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call.

tool_choice.description: Controls which tool is called by the model.

web_search.description: Enable web search for this request.

stream.description: Whether to stream back partial progress.

character_slug.description: The character slug of a public Venice character.

enable_e2ee.description: Enable end-to-end encryption for E2EE-capable models. When true (default), E2EE is used if headers are present. When false, TEE-only mode is used.

enable_web_search.description: Enable web search for this request.

enable_web_scraping.description: Enable Venice web scraping of URLs in the latest user message.

enable_web_citations.description: Request that the LLM cite its sources.

include_venice_system_prompt.description: Whether to include the Venice supplied system prompts.

include_search_results_in_stream.description: Include search results in the stream as the first emitted chunk.

ResponsesRequest.description: Request body for the Responses API endpoint. E2EE-capable models are not supported on /api/v1/responses; use /api/v1/chat/completions with the required E2EE headers instead.
ResponsesRequest.title: Responses API Request

dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.

encoding_format.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either `float` or `base64`.

anyOf.description: The string that will be turned into an embedding. Cannot be an empty string.
anyOf.title: string

anyOf.description: The array of strings that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

anyOf.description: The array of integers that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

anyOf.description: The array of arrays containing integers that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

input.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of tokens. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array of strings or array of token arrays. The input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model (8192 tokens), cannot be an empty string, and any array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
input.example: The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog

model.description: ID of the model to use. You can use the List models API to see all of your available models, or see our Model overview for descriptions of them.

user.description: This is an unused parameter and is discarded by Venice. It is supported solely for API compatibility with OpenAI.

CreateEmbeddingRequestSchema.description: Create embeddings for the supplied input.

example.input: The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog

context_length.description: The context length (maximum input tokens) supported by the model. Only present for text models. This is a standard OpenAI-compatible field that mirrors model_spec.availableContextTokens for client compatibility.

availableContextTokens.description: The context length supported by the model. Only applicable for text models.

maxCompletionTokens.description: The maximum number of completion tokens the model can generate. Use this to know the upper bound for the max_completion_tokens request parameter. Only applicable for text models.

beta.description: Is this model restricted to beta users only? If true, only users with beta access can use this model

betaModel.description: Is this model in beta status?

privacy.description: The privacy mode of the model. Private models have zero data retention. Anonymized models Venice can not guarantee privacy on, but requests are not affiliated with a user

regionRestrictions.description: Country codes where this model is intended to be available. Only present for models with region restrictions metadata.

autoRemap.description: When true, Venice may automatically remap API requests for this model ID to replacementModelId instead of returning an error.

date.description: Legacy ISO 8601 instant aligned with the deprecation sunset used in response headers (`x-venice-model-deprecation-date`). Prefer startsAt / removesAt for new integrations.

removesAt.description: ISO 8601 instant when this model ID is omitted from public GET /models listings (same value as `date` today). Consumers should treat the current wall-clock time as past this instant when deciding whether the model remains listed.

replacementModelId.description: Suggested public API model ID to migrate to, when one exists.

startsAt.description: ISO 8601 instant when deprecation warnings and documentation should be considered active for this model.

deprecation.description: Deprecation information for the model. Only present for models scheduled to be retired

optimizedForCode.description: Is the LLM optimized for coding?

quantization.description: The quantization type of the running model.

supportsFunctionCalling.description: Does the LLM model support function calling?

supportsAudioInput.description: Does the LLM support audio input?

supportsReasoning.description: Does the model support reasoning with <thinking> blocks of output.

supportsReasoningEffort.description: Does the model support the reasoning_effort parameter to control reasoning depth.

reasoningEffortOptions.description: Supported reasoning_effort values for this model. Only present when supportsReasoningEffort is true. The "none" option means reasoning can be disabled.

defaultReasoningEffort.description: Default reasoning_effort value used when the request does not specify one. Only present when supportsReasoningEffort is true.

supportsResponseSchema.description: Does the LLM model support response schema? Only models that support function calling can support response_schema.

supportsMultipleImages.description: Does the LLM support multiple images in a single request? Only applicable when supportsVision is true.

maxImages.description: Maximum number of images supported per request. Only present when supportsMultipleImages is true.

supportsVideoInput.description: Does the LLM support video input?

supportsWebSearch.description: Does the LLM model support web search?

supportsLogProbs.description: Does the LLM model support logprobs parameter?

supportsTeeAttestation.description: Does the model run inside a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) with hardware attestation? When true, use GET /tee/attestation and GET /tee/signature to cryptographically verify that inference occurred inside a genuine TEE.

supportsE2EE.description: Does the model support End-to-End Encryption (E2EE)? When true, clients can encrypt prompts using the TEE public key from attestation, and responses are returned encrypted. Requires supportsTeeAttestation to also be true.

supportsXSearch.description: Does the model support xAI's native X Search (web + X/Twitter search)? When true, you can use venice_parameters.enable_x_search to activate real-time search powered by xAI.

aspectRatios.description: Supported aspect ratio options for this model. Only present for models that support aspect ratio selection.

defaultAspectRatio.description: The default aspect ratio for this model. Only present for models that support aspect ratio selection.

defaultResolution.description: The default resolution for this model. Only present for models that support resolution selection.

resolutions.description: Supported resolution options for this model. Only present for models that support resolution selection.

defaultQuality.description: The default quality for this model. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2).

qualities.description: Supported quality options for this model. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2).

default.description: The default steps value for the model

max.description: The maximum supported steps value for the model

widthHeightDivisor.description: The requested width and height of the image generation must be divisible by this value.

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to image models.
anyOf.title: Image Model Constraints

default.description: The default temperature value for the model
The default top_p value for the model
The default frequency_penalty value for the model
The default presence_penalty value for the model
The default repetition_penalty value for the model

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to text models.
anyOf.title: Text Model Constraints

aspect_ratios.description: The aspect ratios supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined aspect ratio.

resolutions.description: The resolutions supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined resolution.

durations.description: The durations supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined duration.

audio.description: Does the model support audio generation?

audio_configurable.description: Can audio be enabled or disabled for the video generation?

prompt_character_limit.description: The maximum supported prompt length for this video model. If not specified, the default is 2500 characters.

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to video models.
anyOf.title: Video Model Constraints

aspectRatios.description: The aspect ratios supported by this model. Omit the parameter to use the model's default setting.

combineImages.description: Whether this model supports combining multiple input images.

singleImageAspectRatio.description: If false, output dimensions match the input on single-image edits and `aspect_ratio` is ignored. Multi-image edits are unaffected. Defaults to true.

defaultResolution.description: The default resolution for this inpaint model. Only present for models that support resolution selection (e.g. GPT Image 2 Edit).

resolutions.description: Supported resolution options for this inpaint model. Only present for models that support resolution selection.

defaultQuality.description: The default quality for this inpaint model. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2 Edit).

qualities.description: Supported quality options for this inpaint model. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2 Edit).

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to inpaint/edit models.
anyOf.title: Inpaint Model Constraints

constraints.description: Constraints that apply to this model.

description.description: A human-readable description of the model and its capabilities.
description.example: Balanced blend of speed and capability. Handles most everyday tasks with reliability.

modelSource.description: The source of the model, such as a URL to the model repository.

usd.description: USD cost per million input tokens

diem.description: Diem cost per million input tokens

usd.description: USD cost per million cached input tokens (discounted rate for cache reads)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cached input tokens (discounted rate for cache reads)

cache_input.description: Optional pricing for cached input tokens (cache reads). Only present for models that support context caching.

usd.description: USD cost per million cache creation tokens (cache writes). For some providers this may be higher than input price.

diem.description: Diem cost per million cache creation tokens (cache writes). For some providers this may be higher than input price.

cache_write.description: Optional pricing for cache creation tokens (cache writes). Only present for models where provider charges for cache writes (e.g., Anthropic charges 1.25x input price).

usd.description: USD cost per million output tokens

diem.description: Diem cost per million output tokens

context_token_threshold.description: Input token count above which extended pricing applies

usd.description: USD cost per million input tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million input tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million output tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million output tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million cached input tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cached input tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million cache write tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cache write tokens (extended tier)

extended.description: Extended pricing for long-context requests exceeding the threshold. When input tokens exceed context_token_threshold, extended rates apply to the entire request.

anyOf.description: Token-based pricing for chat models. Models supporting context caching will include cache_input (cache read) pricing. Some providers like Anthropic also charge for cache_write (cache creation) at a premium rate.
anyOf.title: LLM Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per image generation (base price)

diem.description: Diem cost per image generation (base price)

generation.description: Base pricing for image generation. Only present for models without resolution-specific pricing.

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

resolutions.description: Resolution-specific pricing. Keys are resolution tiers (e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K"). Only present for models that support resolution selection. When present, replaces `generation`.

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

quality.description: Per-(resolution, quality) pricing. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2 / GPT Image 2 Edit).

usd.description: USD cost for 2x upscale

diem.description: Diem cost for 2x upscale

usd.description: USD cost for 4x upscale

diem.description: Diem cost for 4x upscale

anyOf.description: Pricing for image generation and upscaling
anyOf.title: Image Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per million input characters

diem.description: Diem cost per million input characters

anyOf.description: Pricing for TTS models
anyOf.title: TTS Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per audio second

diem.description: Diem cost per audio second

anyOf.description: Pricing for ASR (speech-to-text) models
anyOf.title: ASR Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per image edit/inpaint operation

diem.description: Diem cost per image edit/inpaint operation

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

resolutions.description: Resolution-specific pricing. Keys are resolution tiers (e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K"). Only present for inpaint models that support resolution selection.

included.description: Number of input images included in the base price. Each additional input image adds the "additional" cost.

usd.description: USD cost per additional input image beyond the included count

diem.description: Diem cost per additional input image beyond the included count

inputImages.description: Input-image pricing. The base price covers "included" input images; each additional input image adds the "additional" cost per output image.

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

usd.description: USD cost for this resolution

diem.description: Diem cost for this resolution

quality.description: Per-(resolution, quality) pricing. Only present for models that accept the `quality` request parameter (currently GPT Image 2 / GPT Image 2 Edit).

anyOf.description: Pricing for image editing/inpainting models
anyOf.title: Inpaint Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per music generation

diem.description: Diem cost per music generation

usd.description: USD cost for this duration tier

diem.description: Diem cost for this duration tier

min_seconds.description: Minimum duration (inclusive) in seconds that falls into this pricing tier

max_seconds.description: Maximum duration (inclusive) in seconds that falls into this pricing tier

usd.description: USD cost per second of generated music

diem.description: Diem cost per second of generated music

usd.description: USD cost per thousand characters

diem.description: Diem cost per thousand characters

traits.description: Traits that apply to this model. You can specify a trait to auto-select a model vs. specifying the model ID in your request to avoid breakage as Venice updates and iterates on its models.

embeddingDimensions.description: The native/default number of dimensions in the output embedding vector. Only present for embedding models.

maxInputTokens.description: Maximum number of input tokens the model accepts per input string. Only present for embedding models.

supportsCustomDimensions.description: Whether the model supports reducing output dimensions via the `dimensions` request parameter. Only present for embedding models that support it.

supports_lyrics.description: Whether this audio-generation model supports lyrics input.

lyrics_required.description: Whether lyrics input is required for this audio-generation model.

supports_force_instrumental.description: Whether this audio-generation model supports the force_instrumental request parameter.

voices.description: The voices available for this model. Applicable for TTS models and voice-enabled music models. Note: each model has its own set of supported voices.

mode.description: How the upstream provider implements voice cloning. `zero_shot` re-reads the reference audio on every synthesis call and never derives a persistent voice template (e.g. Chatterbox HD). `persistent` derives a custom voice template upstream that survives across synthesis calls (e.g. MiniMax Speech-02 HD).

accepted_formats.description: Audio container formats this model accepts as a reference sample on POST /v1/audio/voices. Samples in other containers are rejected with HTTP 400 before any upload.

min_sample_seconds.description: Recommended minimum length of the reference audio sample, in seconds, for an intelligible clone.

retention_days.description: Days a `vv_<id>` voice handle remains valid against this model. For `persistent` models the upstream provider auto-deletes the cloned voice after this many days without use; each successful TTS request resets the window. For `zero_shot` models this is the storage TTL on the uploaded reference audio; the handle stops working when it expires and the user must re-upload.

voice_cloning.description: Voice-cloning capability. Only present for TTS models whose cloning endpoint is available to the caller — pass the model to POST /v1/audio/voices to mint a `vv_<id>` voice handle, then pass that handle back as the `voice` parameter on POST /v1/audio/speech alongside the same `model`.
voice_cloning.title: TTS Voice Cloning

default_voice.description: Default voice for voice-enabled music models.

supports_language_code.description: Whether this music model supports an ISO 639-1 language_code parameter.

supports_speed.description: Whether this music model supports speed adjustment.

default_speed.description: Default speed multiplier for this music model.

min_speed.description: Minimum speed multiplier for this music model.

max_speed.description: Maximum speed multiplier for this music model.

duration_options.description: Available duration options in seconds for this music model.

min_duration.description: Minimum duration in seconds for this music model.

max_duration.description: Maximum duration in seconds for this music model.

default_duration.description: Default duration in seconds for this music model.

supported_formats.description: Supported audio formats for this music model.

default_format.description: Default audio format for this music model.

prompt_character_limit.description: Maximum prompt character limit for this music model.

min_prompt_length.description: Minimum prompt length for this music model.

lyrics_character_limit.description: Maximum lyrics character limit for this music model.

model_spec.description: Compact and efficient model for quick responses and lighter workloads.
model_spec.name: Llama 3.2 3B
